column1: # Vat.gr.1291, f.6v — Ptolemy, Handy Tables: Oblique Ascensions for Klima V (Byzantion, longest day = 15h)
# Last 3 signs of the zodiac: ΑΙΓΟΚΕΡΩ (Capricorn), ὙΔΡΗΧΟΟΥ (Aquarius), ἸΧΘΥΩΝ (Pisces)
# Values continue cumulatively from end of Sagittarius (201;59° on f.6r)
# Expected end-of-Pisces value: 360° (full celestial sphere rotation)
# Expected: at end of Pisces, cumulative ascension MUST equal 360° (full rotation of celestial sphere)
# My total reading: 286;39° — only ~287° instead of 360°
# This means there are systematic transcription errors somewhere across the 12 signs,
# OR I am misunderstanding the tabular convention used here.
# Most likely cause: misreading of the leading sigma/tau prefix in some rows.
# In bisantine numerals: σ = 200, τ = 300, υ = 400. If some "σ" rows are actually "τ",
# the values would jump up appropriately.
# This is EXACTLY the kind of systematic error your ML pipeline should auto-detect:
# - Total-rotation check (sum to 360°) at end of zodiac
# - Differential check (each sign should contribute ~30° on average with seasonal variation)
# These checks alone would flag the problem without manual review.
